body:
## Context

`.ssot/registry.json` is the canonical machine-readable SSOT source. The repository also writes and stores additional JSON artifacts under `.ssot/` (schemas, reports, snapshots, exports, and evidence bundles).

Existing JSON output is stable but not guaranteed to follow RFC 8785 JSON Canonicalization Scheme (JCS), which leaves canonical byte representation and cross-runtime hashing behavior underspecified.

## Decision

ssot-core adopts RFC 8785 JSON Canonicalization Scheme (JCS) as the canonical JSON format for SSOT JSON artifacts.

- `.ssot/registry.json` SHALL be serialized as RFC 8785 JCS canonical JSON.
- JSON files under `.ssot/` SHALL be serialized as RFC 8785 JCS canonical JSON.
- Validation SHALL fail when any `.ssot/*.json` content is not canonical RFC 8785 JCS JSON.

ADR and SPEC authored documents remain YAML/JSON document payloads governed by existing document rules; this decision applies to JSON artifact canonicalization under `.ssot/`.

## Consequences

JSON writes in ssot-core must use a single canonical serializer.

Validation gains deterministic checks for JCS canonical formatting and duplicate-key/non-finite-number rejection in `.ssot/*.json`.

Hashes and diffs across platforms become deterministic for canonical JSON artifacts under `.ssot/`.
summary: `.ssot/registry.json` is the canonical machine-readable SSOT source. The repository also writes and stores additional JSON artifacts under `.ssot/` (schemas, reports, snapshots, exports, and evidence bundles).
title: Adopt RFC 8785 JCS for .ssot JSON artifacts
